outputs.main.sas:
library(admiral)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(haven)
library(xportr)
library(metacore)
library(metatools)

# Read SDTM datasets
adsl <- read_xpt("sdtm/adsl.xpt")
lb <- read_xpt("sdtm/lb.xpt")

# Read metadata
metacore <- spec_to_metacore("metadata/specs.xlsx", where_sep_sheet = FALSE, quiet = TRUE)

# Derive ADSL variables needed
adsl <- adsl %>%
  select(STUDYID, USUBJID, SITEID, AGE, AGEU, SEX, RACE, ETHNIC, 
         COUNTRY, ARM, ACTARM, ARMCD, ACTARMCD, TRT01P, TRT01A, 
         TRTSDT, TRTEDT, RFSTDTC, RFENDTC, SAFFL, ITTFL)

# Filter LB for lab tests and merge with ADSL
adlbc <- lb %>%
  filter(LBBLFL == "Y" | !is.na(VISITNUM)) %>%
  derive_vars_merged(
    dataset_add = adsl,
    by_vars = exprs(STUDYID, USUBJID),
    new_vars = exprs(
      SITEID, AGE, AGEU, SEX, RACE, ETHNIC, COUNTRY,
      ARM, ACTARM, ARMCD, ACTARMCD, TRT01P, TRT01A,
      TRTSDT, TRTEDT, SAFFL, ITTFL
    )
  )

# Derive PARAMCD, PARAM, PARAMN
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  mutate(
    PARAMCD = LBTESTCD,
    PARAM = LBTEST,
    PARAMN = as.numeric(as.factor(PARAMCD))
  )

# Derive AVAL from LBSTRESN
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  mutate(AVAL = LBSTRESN)

# Derive ADT, ADY
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  derive_vars_dt(
    new_vars_prefix = "A",
    dtc = LBDTC
  ) %>%
  derive_vars_dy(
    reference_date = TRTSDT,
    source_vars = exprs(ADT)
  )

# Derive ANRLO, ANRHI
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  mutate(
    ANRLO = LBSTNRLO,
    ANRHI = LBSTNRHI
  )

# Derive ANRIND (Analysis Reference Range Indicator)
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  mutate(
    ANRIND = case_when(
      is.na(AVAL) ~ NA_character_,
      is.na(ANRLO) & is.na(ANRHI) ~ NA_character_,
      !is.na(ANRLO) & !is.na(ANRHI) & AVAL >= ANRLO & AVAL <= ANRHI ~ "NORMAL",
      !is.na(ANRHI) & AVAL > ANRHI ~ "HIGH",
      !is.na(ANRLO) & AVAL < ANRLO ~ "LOW",
      !is.na(ANRLO) & is.na(ANRHI) & AVAL >= ANRLO ~ "NORMAL",
      !is.na(ANRHI) & is.na(ANRLO) & AVAL <= ANRHI ~ "NORMAL",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

# Derive BASE and BASETYPE
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  derive_var_base(
    by_vars = exprs(STUDYID, USUBJID, PARAMCD),
    source_var = AVAL,
    new_var = BASE,
    filter = LBBLFL == "Y"
  )

adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  mutate(BASETYPE = if_else(!is.na(BASE), "LAST", NA_character_))

# Derive BNRIND (Baseline Reference Range Indicator)
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  derive_var_base(
    by_vars = exprs(STUDYID, USUBJID, PARAMCD),
    source_var = ANRIND,
    new_var = BNRIND,
    filter = LBBLFL == "Y"
  )

# Derive CHG and PCHG
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  derive_var_chg() %>%
  derive_var_pchg()

# Derive SHIFT variable
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  mutate(
    SHIFT1 = case_when(
      is.na(BNRIND) | is.na(ANRIND) ~ NA_character_,
      BNRIND == "LOW" & ANRIND == "LOW" ~ "L to L",
      BNRIND == "LOW" & ANRIND == "NORMAL" ~ "L to N",
      BNRIND == "LOW" & ANRIND == "HIGH" ~ "L to H",
      BNRIND == "NORMAL" & ANRIND == "LOW" ~ "N to L",
      BNRIND == "NORMAL" & ANRIND == "NORMAL" ~ "N to N",
      BNRIND == "NORMAL" & ANRIND == "HIGH" ~ "N to H",
      BNRIND == "HIGH" & ANRIND == "LOW" ~ "H to L",
      BNRIND == "HIGH" & ANRIND == "NORMAL" ~ "H to N",
      BNRIND == "HIGH" & ANRIND == "HIGH" ~ "H to H",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

# Derive AVISIT, AVISITN
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  mutate(
    AVISIT = VISIT,
    AVISITN = VISITNUM
  )

# Derive ABLFL
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  mutate(ABLFL = LBBLFL)

# Derive ANL01FL for post-baseline records
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  mutate(
    ANL01FL = case_when(
      !is.na(AVAL) & !is.na(BASE) & ABLFL != "Y" ~ "Y",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

# Add DTYPE
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  mutate(DTYPE = NA_character_)

# Derive analysis sequence number
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  group_by(STUDYID, USUBJID, PARAMCD) %>%
  mutate(ASEQ = row_number()) %>%
  ungroup()

# Select and order variables
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  select(
    STUDYID, USUBJID, SUBJID = USUBJID, SITEID,
    AGE, AGEU, SEX, RACE, ETHNIC, COUNTRY,
    ARM, ACTARM, ARMCD, ACTARMCD, TRT01P, TRT01A,
    TRTSDT, TRTEDT, SAFFL, ITTFL,
    PARAMCD, PARAM, PARAMN,
    AVAL, AVALC = LBSTRESC, BASE, BASETYPE, ABLFL, BNRIND,
    CHG, PCHG, ANRIND, SHIFT1,
    ADT, ADY, AVISIT, AVISITN,
    LBSEQ, ASEQ, ANL01FL, DTYPE,
    ANRLO, ANRHI, LBCAT, LBSCAT, LBSPEC, LBMETHOD
  )

# Apply variable labels
var_labels <- c(
  STUDYID = "Study Identifier",
  USUBJID = "Unique Subject Identifier",
  SUBJID = "Subject Identifier for the Study",
  SITEID = "Study Site Identifier",
  AGE = "Age",
  AGEU = "Age Units",
  SEX = "Sex",
  RACE = "Race",
  ETHNIC = "Ethnicity",
  COUNTRY = "Country",
  ARM = "Description of Planned Arm",
  ACTARM = "Description of Actual Arm",
  ARMCD = "Planned Arm Code",
  ACTARMCD = "Actual Arm Code",
  TRT01P = "Planned Treatment for Period 01",
  TRT01A = "Actual Treatment for Period 01",
  TRTSDT = "Date of First Exposure to Treatment",
  TRTEDT = "Date of Last Exposure to Treatment",
  SAFFL = "Safety Population Flag",
  ITTFL = "Intent-To-Treat Population Flag",
  PARAMCD = "Parameter Code",
  PARAM = "Parameter",
  PARAMN = "Parameter Number",
  AVAL = "Analysis Value",
  AVALC = "Analysis Value (C)",
  BASE = "Baseline Value",
  BASETYPE = "Baseline Type",
  ABLFL = "Baseline Record Flag",
  BNRIND = "Baseline Reference Range Indicator",
  CHG = "Change from Baseline",
  PCHG = "Percent Change from Baseline",
  ANRIND = "Analysis Reference Range Indicator",
  SHIFT1 = "Shift from Baseline to Analysis",
  ADT = "Analysis Date",
  ADY = "Analysis Relative Day",
  AVISIT = "Analysis Visit",
  AVISITN = "Analysis Visit (N)",
  LBSEQ = "Sequence Number",
  ASEQ = "Analysis Sequence Number",
  ANL01FL = "Analysis Flag 01",
  DTYPE = "Derivation Type",
  ANRLO = "Analysis Normal Range Lower Limit",
  ANRHI = "Analysis Normal Range Upper Limit",
  LBCAT = "Category for Lab Test",
  LBSCAT = "Subcategory for Lab Test",
  LBSPEC = "Specimen Type",
  LBMETHOD = "Method of Test or Examination"
)

for (var in names(var_labels)) {
  if (var %in% names(adlbc)) {
    attr(adlbc[[var]], "label") <- var_labels[[var]]
  }
}

# Write XPT file
write_xpt(
  adlbc,
  path = "adam/adlbc.xpt",
  label = "Analysis Dataset Lab Blood Chemistry Shift",
  strict_checks = FALSE
)
